query: LLMs, Marked is the ultimate Markdown preview complaints OR frustrating OR broken

results.title: Suggested strategies for "skipping" incomplete/malformed ... - GitHub
results.content:
your responses are unhelpful and do not address the original question

The original question has the problem backwards, and is a non goal for the project.  
We parse standard markdown , we're happy to offer advise on how to generate valid markdown, but have no intent to try to handle invalid content in a way that goes against the specifications.

unhelpful, you're just complaining about how LLMs generate markdown

It isn't a complaint so much as an observation.  
At the end of the day, you own your content creation pipeline, if your human/ai/whatever authors generate invalid content. That is a problem of the pipeline, not of markdown.

My suggestions are towards ways that the problem can be solved, which is addressing the issue where it originates, in the pipeline. [...] | your responses are unhelpful and do not address the original question  The original question has the problem backwards, and is a non goal for the project.  We parse standard markdown , we're happy to offer advise on how to generate valid markdown, but have no intent to try to handle invalid content in a way that goes against the specifications.  unhelpful, you're just complaining about how LLMs generate markdown  It isn't a complaint so much as an observation.  At the end of the day, you own your content creation pipeline, if your human/ai/whatever authors generate invalid content. That is a problem of the pipeline, not of markdown.  My suggestions are towards ways that the problem can be solved, which is addressing the issue where it originates, in the pipeline.  the pragmatic solution [...] This is a misunderstanding. The LLMs do produce valid markdown. The problem is how to render/pre-render markdown content coming chunk by chunk (streamed), which is naturally becoming valid and invalid, before it becomes complete.

No misunderstanding. I understand where you are coming from a UX perspective. I am communicating to you and others that distinction does not exist at a CommonMark language level.

This is a dream of every front-end developer. A service, which returns the complete data instantaneously :-) But while we're awake, we build workarounds for the real-world services :-)

There are already faster options than next token language models.  
Diffusion LLMs being the thing to keep an eye on.

results.title: The “Broken” Markdown: Why I Built a Specialized LLM-Ready Viewer
results.content:
## The Problem I Faced

If you have ever integrated an LLM API into a backend system or worked with raw JSON responses, you’ve seen the “ugly” version of Markdown generated by LLMssss. While chat interfaces like Gemini or ChatGPT render everything beautifully, the actual data being sent over the wire often looks like this

### Top Recommendations\n\n1.  Python : High versatility.\t- Used for AI.\n2.  Rust : Memory safety.
When you paste that into a standard Markdown editor (like VS Code, Obsidian, or a basic online renderer), it breaks. Standard renderers expect literal newlines (actual hits of the Enter key). They don’t know what to do with `\n` or `\t.` [...] Sitemap

Open in app

Sign up

Sign in

. But there is a silent, friction filled problem that occurs the moment we try to take that AI output and put it into a standard Markdown renderer to preview. Yes, I am talking about he “The Escape Character Nightmare”.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Image 3

Source: Image by AI

So I gave this problem to the Claude and within 3 mins it built this Markdown Viewer because I was tired of “cleaning” my text before I could actually read it. This isn’t a complex enterprise platform it’s a simple utility built to solve a specific, recurring headache.

## The Problem I Faced [...] Normal Mode: For the classic Markdown use case. If you’re drafting a `README.md`, it behaves exactly like you’d expect. It’s clean, fast, and uses high-fidelity syntax highlighting.
   LLM Mode (The “Sanity” Toggle): This is the heart of the project. It applies a pre-processing layer that scans the input for escaped sequences the `\n`and converts them into visual formatting _before_ passing them to the parser. It transforms a "data string" into a "document" instantly.

## Insights

We often think that “building” in the AI space means training models or creating massive platforms. But sometimes, the most useful thing you can build is a bridge.

results.title: Marked 3 (Markdown previewer) - Software - MPU Talk
results.content:
Agreed. I bought version 2 less than a month ago and was really a fan but I abandoned it after two weeks and am now using Obsidian instead. My main complaint was that there was no real docking for Marked 2 with BBEdit but I simulated some automatic side-by-side placement logic using Keyboard Maestro with some Applescript. I think it’s a tad overpriced myself (for my purposes) but I’m sure that it’s worth it to the right kind of user who will take advantage of the features. I would have liked the Scrivener feature but I abandoned that program months ago. I had lots of docs in Pages so I like a conversion utility for that but I don’t remember that being part of the feature set - and I’ve converted those manually myself, I’d sill like to have Marked 3 though for the Schema ripping feature. [...] MPU Talk

# Marked 3 (Markdown previewer)

Brett Terpstra did a soft launch of Marked 3 today:

### Marked 3 (@marked@indieapps.space)

Marked 3 is officially available on all channels, though I'm holding off on a big post/newsletter about it until the next version gets approved in the MAS with bugfixes. But you can download from the Mac App Store, Setapp, or direct from...

### Marked - The Ultimate Markdown Preview and Converter

Marked is a powerful markdown preview and converter. It supports HTML, PDF, DOCX, and more.

On macOS $30/year or $70 permanent unlock.

For a markdown previewer/exporter – pretty steep.

Katie

I like whatever version I’m using with SetApp (2 I assume) but I can’t imagine paying £30 a year for it. [...] But it does its job well and is even more helpful in this AI era of lots of markdown files. Assuming 3 is an improvement, could well be handy for some.

By the way I combine it with OpenIn so that when I open a md file I can choose to edit it in MarkedIt or view it Marked. Works really well, better than a split view app imo.

Version 3 is in Setapp now and should be automatically updated – at least mine was… I haven’t tried it out yet though.

Version 3 is in Setapp now

:+1:

:+1:

Works fine

Katie

On macOS $30/year or $70 permanent unlock.

For a markdown previewer/exporter – pretty steep.

As a beta tester, I received a 50% discount for the lifetime option, but even €39,99 (the full price is €79,99 in Europe) is still pretty steep… (IMHO)

results.title: Marked 3 — Markdown Preview for Mac
results.content:
### Essential in my workflow

> I generate and work with a lot of markdown files. There are similar editors and viewers. Marked is the best one I've found. The developer is highly responsive to bugs improvement ideas.

> Per-document memory that survives a move is the exact thing I was missing. I keep a style per publish target, and the app-wide switch was my one real gripe, so that's solved. Going to put the Style…

> I have been waiting for this great upgrade to Marked. I really can't manage my work without it. It does far more than I can manage, but this new version makes a significant improvement to my…

### Most excellent Markdown Viewer

> The always improving Markdown viewer. Long time user, highly recommended; Love Marked 3.

Marked 3 delivers a superior Markdown preview experience for Mac with advanced proofreading tools, beautiful themes, and powerful export options.

results.title: ‎Marked 2 - Markdown Preview App - App Store
results.content:
dsparky

  Sure, it has great Markdown previews, but it really shines because of its well-explained and well-organized options. Exploring them can unlock capabilities you probably don't know about: equations, text folding, application integrations, and proofing. Brett has continually tended to Marked for many years and it shows. Whether reading alongside your primary editor or exporting, you won't be disappointed. [...] Apple Dispatch

  Markdown is the simple way to format your plain text quickly and efficiently for whatever your ultimate destination may be. And Marked 2 is the perfect companion app to display your Markdown output, export it to other formats like rich text and pdf, or to print out your writing for further review or distribution. It does all this and more in whatever writing application you choose to use. The developer is also constantly improving the app by adding new features and ensuring that it functions flawlessly.Marked puts you in charge of Markdown on the Mac!
 ### Great app - unless you are using Ulysses - Updated

  Rick in AL [...] Updated response: Ulysses previews simply require using ⌘6 and pressing return once you set Marked up as the external editor. This is clearly documented in the help.I'm uncertain where you got the idea that 14-day trials were an option on the Mac App Store. They are not, unless I switch to a subscription model. The trial is available via the website and, as stated, always has been.\\\I'm happy to let you know that there is and has always been a free trial of Marked 2 at  As detailed in your support request, Marked supports Ulysses to the fullest extent possible. Ulysses does not provide text files on disk that Marked can read, but does provide full previews via their quick export (⌘6) preview. I'm sorry to see that you've chosen to see this as a shortcoming in Marked.
